project.name: Sovereign 7G Network
project.description: DePIN settlement layer for AI agents. 62 autonomous agents. 7 ecosystem integrations. Cross-chain settlement. Quantum timing.

nodes.name: fabrika_api.py
nodes.summary: Sovereign FABRIKA Engine API — dual-token hardware synthesis with FABRIKAEngine, HilbertSystem, PhotonicSystem, and FoundryAudit for manufacturing CNC/3D-printable aerospace components. Integrates ICP anchoring and verification.

nodes.name: axon.ts
nodes.summary: Axon sovereign orchestrator in TypeScript — analog to Vite in the Voidzero Trinity. Discovers modules, extracts imports/exports, resolves trust boundaries, calls Zetto for linking, and manages plugin pipelines.

nodes.name: zetto_bridge.ts
nodes.summary: Zero-copy FFI bridge from Axon (TypeScript/Bun) to Zetto (Rust cdylib) via Bun's native bun:ffi. Handles shared library discovery, type marshaling, and function binding for the full C-ABI surface.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Zetto sovereign module linker in Rust — tree-shakes, links, and seals modules with Keccak-256. Exposes C-ABI FFI for zero-copy consumption by Axon (Bun). Manages module graph, trust boundaries, and sealed output chunks.

nodes.name: shm.rs
nodes.summary: POSIX shared memory bridge for zero-copy data exchange between Zetto (Rust) and Mojo SIMD kernels. Fixed-layout header + node types/values/attest-hash arrays enabling direct memory access without serialization.

nodes.name: zetto_bridge.py
nodes.summary: Python ctypes bridge to libzetto.so (Rust cdylib) — eliminates subprocess/HTTP boundary between Python orchestration and the Rust linker. Provides init, add_module, link, seal, and version methods with zero serialization overhead.

nodes.name: MERIENDA_DEPIN_ORCHESTRATOR.py
nodes.summary: DePIN fabrication orchestrator for the Merienda protocol — priority job queue with Genesis/Platinum/Gold/Silver/Bronze tiers, SQLite persistence, dual-token ($HIL + Merit) settlement, and social multiplier computation.

nodes.name: benchmark_speculative.py
nodes.summary: Speculative benchmark suite — measures the performance impact of speculative execution, prefetching, and branch prediction optimizations on the Sovereign OS infrastructure.

nodes.name: bifrost_execution_bridge.py
nodes.summary: Secure Web3 bridge linking portfolio allocations to on-chain execution — integrates web3, CCTP, FedNow, Avalanche L1, Mercury API, Custodia, Nium, OpenTrade RWA bridges with Raft consensus guard and topological similarity engine.

nodes.name: embedding_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Era II semantic search pipeline — generates 384-dim all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings on every write_concept via ChromaDB (persistent, file-based, zero external services). Upgrades search from grep-based to cosine similarity vector search.

nodes.name: era2_phase2.py
nodes.summary: Era II Phase 2 — OKF knowledge graph built from embedding similarity, recommender system, semantic dashboard data endpoint, and multi-concept reasoning engine. Builds relationship graphs, recommends concepts, and synthesizes insights across concepts.

nodes.name: era3_adaptive.py
nodes.summary: Era III Adaptive Layer — auto-type generator discovers new concept types, skill evolution tracks usage and proposes improvements, self-healing engine detects anomalies and spawns repairs, feedback learner captures human edits as training data.

nodes.name: era4_collaborative.py
nodes.summary: Era IV Collaborative Layer — federated OKF sync across instances via git remotes, agent swarm spawning specialized agents with task assignment, knowledge market for publishing/acquiring concepts, and collective reasoning across instances.

nodes.name: era5_autonomous.py
nodes.summary: Era V Autonomous Layer — self-improvement analyzes own code with rollback, ethics layer constrains autonomous action with human oversight, continuous evolution never-done monitoring and improvement loop. All actions logged to OKF.

nodes.name: external_gateway.py
nodes.summary: Zero-trust external model gateway — routes queries to DeepSeek, Kimi, TextCortex with sensitive data pattern filtering, stable prompt prefix for caching, response audit trail, and policy-based access control.

nodes.name: magix_okf.py
nodes.summary: Unified OKF (Open Knowledge Federation) module — the core knowledge engine serving concept read/write/list on port 9002. Merges okf_bridge and kiro_mcp, integrates with s2l_pipeline, embedding_pipeline, external_gateway, and antigravity_benchmark.

nodes.name: okf_validator.py
nodes.summary: SCHEMA.md-based validator for OKF concepts — parses type definitions from SCHEMA.md, generates JSON Schema, and validates frontmatter on every write_concept call with fallback schemas for KnowledgeBundle, ResearchFinding, EmailThread and more.

nodes.name: s2l_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Skill-to-LoRA training and inference engine — generates training datasets from OKF concepts, manages 6 skill adapters (research, email, document, compare, calendar, notes), hot-swaps adapters, and routes inference through hybrid pipeline.

nodes.name: semantic_search_cache.py
nodes.summary: Dual-index session search combining SQLite FTS5 full-text keyword search with LanceDB-like vector embedding similarity. Sessions indexed on save, retrievable via either method or merged ranking.

nodes.name: token_optimizer.py
nodes.summary: Token cost optimization for Hermes Desktop + DeepSeek — 5 strategies: prompt prefix stabilization for DeepSeek caching (98% cheaper), retrieval-based memory injection (72% reduction), semantic caching (30-60% hit rate), context compression (80-95%), and prompt structure optimization.

nodes.name: triad_dashboard.py
nodes.summary: Live monitoring dashboard for the Sovereign OS Triad (Antigravity, Hermes, Odysseus) — dark-themed web UI on port 8080 with real-time metrics polling every 5 seconds via TriadMetrics collector and OKF bundle state.

nodes.name: triad_metrics.py
nodes.summary: Metrics collector for the Sovereign OS Triad — collects live state from Antigravity, Hermes, Odysseus, OKF bundle, S2L pipeline, and external gateway. Tracks request counts, latencies, errors, and system-specific metrics.

nodes.name: vision_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Era VI vision pipeline — extends embedding pipeline with CLIP-based image embeddings, creating OKF ImageConcept types searchable alongside text concepts. Zero-trust design with hash-based fallback.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Agent package initializer — exports agent classes and provides path configuration for the agents package.

nodes.name: acp_discovery.py
nodes.summary: ACP Discovery Endpoint — authenticated, rate-limited HTTP server exposing agent capabilities, webhooks, and monitoring for the Agent Communication Protocol. Integrates with AetherDB canister for event logging.

nodes.name: chatwoot_support.py
nodes.summary: Agent 74 — ChatwootSupportAgent connecting the S7G agent swarm to Chatwoot's omni-channel inbox. Monitors support tickets, auto-responds to common queries (settlement, yield, security), and escalates complex issues to human operators.

nodes.name: content_generator.py
nodes.summary: Content generation agent — produces marketing copy, technical documentation, and social media content using local LLM inference, integrated with the Mautic campaign engine.

nodes.name: event_fabric.py
nodes.summary: Event Fabric agent — cross-ecosystem event routing across 7 platforms (BlockRun, vn.py, Eve, Qlib, Chatwoot, Mautic, PingCRM), normalizing events into a unified format.

nodes.name: hermes_agents.py
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper module re-exporting HermesSecurityAuditor, HermesCostOptimizer, HermesCapacityPlanner, and HermesEnvironmentManager from their individual agent modules.

nodes.name: knowledge_base.py
nodes.summary: Agent 75 — KnowledgeBaseAgent that syncs S7G documentation to Chatwoot Help Center, updates knowledge base with new features, and tracks effectiveness. Manages 6+ knowledge articles across getting-started, settlement, yield, and security categories.

nodes.name: llm_decision_maker.py
nodes.summary: LLM-powered decision engine for Hermes agents — routes prompts to local sovereign router (port 9877/4000) or OpenAI fallback, parses JSON-structured decisions, handles authentication across endpoints.

nodes.name: mautic_campaign.py
nodes.summary: Mautic campaign agent — manages 5 campaign templates across email, SMS, and web push channels, automates A/B testing, and tracks campaign performance metrics.

nodes.name: mautic_lead.py
nodes.summary: Mautic lead management agent — scores leads from 6 sources, applies 6 scoring rules, creates segments, and triggers campaign actions based on lead behavior and demographics.

nodes.name: pingcrm_integration.py
nodes.summary: PingCRM integration agent — manages 5 contact types, syncs contact data with Telegram, and bridges CRM operations with the S7G settlement and support systems.

nodes.name: swarm.py
nodes.summary: Agent Swarm Coordinator — orchestrates 17+ specialized AI agents for autonomous liquidity management, arbitrage, yield harvesting, and network operations with configurable execution intervals (5s to 86400s). Includes LiquidityProvider, Arbitrage, YieldHarvester, and specialized DeFi agents.

nodes.name: swarm_orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Swarm orchestrator — self-healing coordinator that auto-restarts failed agents, rebalances load across the swarm, and manages agent registration/deregistration.

nodes.name: axon_broadway_poc.py
nodes.summary: Axon Broadway Pipeline (Phases 2 & 3) — FastAPI WebSocket server streaming SQLite WAL node telemetry and Keccak consensus metrics. Integrates Zetto attestation, CORS middleware, and HTML5 canvas client for the Broadway GDK WebSocket ingress.

nodes.name: agent_event_bus.py
nodes.summary: In-process publish/subscribe event bus for agent-to-agent communication — threadsafe with AetherDB persistence, polling reader for cross-process events, and pre-registered default event chains (security_alert, yield_update, bridge_settlement, agent_action).

nodes.name: anomaly_detector.py
nodes.summary: Anomaly detection engine — applies statistical methods and ML models to detect unusual patterns in agent behavior, network traffic, and system metrics. Triggers alerts and auto-responses.

nodes.name: discord_bot.py
nodes.summary: Discord bot for S7G monitoring — provides Discord-based alerting and status reporting as an alternative notification channel alongside Telegram.

nodes.name: monitor_bot.py
nodes.summary: Unified monitoring bot — aggregates all monitoring channels (Telegram, Discord, webhooks) into a single interface with configurable alert routing, deduplication, and escalation policies.

nodes.name: pos_bridge.py
nodes.summary: POS dongle-to-S7G integration bridge — receives Magix POS settlement webhooks (Zavo, Razorpay, Airwallex), routes to S7G YieldRouter, stores Keccak proofs in aetherdb, and submits via cross-chain relayer. Supports 5 corridors and HSE TernaryKeccak signing.

nodes.name: relayer.py
nodes.summary: Cross-chain relayer process — polls source chains for cross-chain messages, submits merkle proofs to destination chains, and manages the relayer queue with retry logic.

nodes.name: relayer_ha.py
nodes.summary: Relayer high-availability monitor — watches cross-chain relayer processes, detects failures, and triggers failover to backup relayers to ensure continuous cross-chain message delivery.

nodes.name: rfi_monitor.py
nodes.summary: RFI (Request for Information) monitor — watches for incoming RFI requests from partner networks and ecosystem integrations, auto-categorizes them, and routes to appropriate agents.

nodes.name: telegram_bot.py
nodes.summary: Telegram bot for real-time swarm alerts and commands — sends alerts (discovery, payment, error), provides /status command, broadcasts to Discord via webhook, and supports multi-platform notification routing.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Sovereign 7G Python SDK v2.4 entry point — 83 methods across 5 modules covering voice, messaging, nodes, beamforming, analytics, identity, LoRA IoT, ICP mesh, cross-chain settlement, and AI spec KB. Configures S7GClient with API base, ICP calls, SIP messaging, and all domain-specific operations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Domains subpackage init for the S7G SDK — consolidates domain-specific module exports.

nodes.name: agents.py
nodes.summary: Agent swarm SDK methods — swarm status (62 agents), agent detail, log retrieval, and constants for agents, courts, stablecoins, and supported chains.

nodes.name: bridge.py
nodes.summary: CCTP bridge, POS settlement, and court operations SDK methods — POS settle/verify/status, court executive pause/freeze, arbitration file/status across the S7G governance framework.

nodes.name: solana.py
nodes.summary: Solana integration methods for the S7G SDK — validator info, staking/unstaking, swaps via Jupiter, balance checks, transfers, MoneyGram tracking, network stats, and DePIN project queries.

nodes.name: yield_router.py
nodes.summary: YieldRouter vault method — single function for MultiYieldVault operations (deposit, withdraw, check yields) across supported stablecoins.

nodes.name: test_session_comprehensive.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive session test suite — 19 tests covering agents (swarm, ACP, knowledge base, decision maker), infrastructure (pipeline, validators), stress/soak, and tracing scenarios with pass/fail reporting.

nodes.name: zetto_attestation.py
nodes.summary: Zetto attestation adapter — drop-in replacement for os.urandom-based attestation hashes with Zetto's Keccak-256 seals. Provides sovereign_attest, seal_transaction, and ZettoAttester with graceful fallback to Python hashlib.

layers.name: Infrastructure Core
layers.description: Core engine layer — OKF (Open Knowledge Federation) runtime, multi-era adaptive/autonomous/collaborative systems, embedding pipeline, S2L training, quantum-safe primitives, zero-trust security, monitoring dashboards, and the DePIN orchestrator. This is the foundation that all other layers build upon.

layers.name: Agent System
layers.description: Autonomous agent swarm — 62 specialized agents covering DeFi (liquidity, yield, arbitrage), security (SIEM, threat intel, bridge monitors), orchestration, ecosystem integrations (BlockRun, vn.py, Eve, Qlib, Chatwoot, Mautic, PingCRM), telephony, and infrastructure management. Agents run on configurable intervals from sub-second to daily.

layers.name: Substrate Layer
layers.description: Low-level compiled substrate — Rust (Zetto) module linker/tree-shaker with Keccak-256 sealing, POSIX shared memory bridge for Mojo zero-copy integration, TypeScript (Axon) sovereign orchestrator and FFI bridge to Zetto, and the FABRIKA hardware synthesis API for dual-token settlement on the physical manufacturing substrate.

layers.name: SDK
layers.description: Python SDK v2.4 — unified client for the S7G network providing 83 methods across 5 modules covering voice, messaging, nodes, beamforming, analytics, identity, LoRA IoT, ICP mesh, cross-chain settlement, Solana validator interactions, yield routing, and agent swarm queries.

layers.name: Bridge & Event Bus
layers.description: Inter-process communication layer — in-process pub/sub agent event bus with AetherDB persistence, CCTP cross-chain USDC bridge, and event pipeline for cross-process discovery. Routes events between agents, on-chain settlement, and external systems.

layers.name: Monitoring & Alerting
layers.description: System monitoring and alerting — POS settlement bridge webhook handler, Telegram/Discord bots for real-time swarm alerts and queries, anomaly detection, relayer health monitoring, RFI monitor, MoneyGram validator tracker, and systemd-service aware status reporting.

layers.name: Root-level Modules & Scripts
layers.description: Top-level entrypoints and orchestration — sovereign CLI, agent runtime, SIP/WebRTC telephony gateway, x402 payment middleware, Zetto attestation adapter, beam controller, Mojo SIMD photonic beamforming, core handlers, routing, roles, memory, semantic graph, workflow discovery, and Axel Broadway pipeline.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Automated test suite — comprehensive session tests, cross-chain flow integration tests, feature integration tests, stress testing, chaos testing, AI feedback validation, distributed tracing tests, and soak tests. All 19 core tests passing.

steps.rationale: The absolute foundation — Zetto's Rust linker defines the core abstraction for the entire system: a module graph that tree-shakes, links, and Keccak-256 seals sovereign modules via C-ABI FFI. Understanding the SovereignModule/SealedChunk/LinkResult data model and ModuleGraph's trust-boundary-aware linking is essential before any higher layer makes sense. This is the 'kernel' that every other component ultimately depends on.
focusAreas: SovereignModule struct — what constitutes a 'module' in this architecture
ModuleGraph — how trust boundaries are resolved during linking
KeccakAttestationEngine — why Keccak-256 (not SHA-3) was chosen for sealing
C-ABI surface — what functions are exposed for zero-copy consumption by TypeScript/Python

steps.rationale: POSIX shared memory bridge between Rust (Zetto) and Mojo SIMD kernels — a zero-copy IPC design that avoids serialization overhead entirely. The ShmHeader + fixed-layout arrays design is the key architectural decision for high-throughput photonic beam steering. Understanding this explains the 'zero-cost infrastructure' claim at the substrate level.
focusAreas: ShmHeader layout — fixed-layout IPC contract design
SharedAstRegion — how zero-copy memory sharing works across runtimes
ShmStatus enum — state machine for shared memory lifecycle
Why POSIX shm vs pipes/sockets/HTTP for cross-runtime communication

steps.rationale: The Python FFI bridge to libzetto.so via ctypes — eliminates subprocess/HTTP boundary between the Python orchestration layer and Rust substrate. The architectural insight is that every layer above this (agents, SDK, scripts) ultimately calls through this bridge. Understanding the _benchmark method and ZettoBridge class APIs reveals the performance-sensitive seam in the architecture.
focusAreas: ctypes binding pattern — how C-ABI functions are marshalled
ZettoBridge API surface — init/add_module/link/seal as the canonical 4-step pipeline
Zero-serialization design — why Python talks to Rust via FFI, not REST/gRPC
Benchmarking architecture — how the bridge self-measures overhead

steps.rationale: The TypeScript/Bun FFI bridge to Zetto via bun:ffi — parallel to the Python bridge but for the Axon orchestrator. The architectural pattern of maintaining two language bridges (Python + TypeScript) to the same Rust core is a deliberate polyglot design. Understanding the findZettoLib discovery logic and type marshaling reveals how the system handles cross-language type safety.
focusAreas: bun:ffi usage — native FFI vs. child_process/subprocess patterns
Shared library discovery — findZettoLib path resolution strategy
SovereignModule/SealedChunk/LinkResult interface types across both bridges
Why both Python and TypeScript bridges exist (different consuming layers)

steps.rationale: The Axon orchestrator — analog to Vite in the Voidzero Trinity. This TypeScript module defines the plugin pipeline architecture: scanning modules, extracting imports/exports, resolving trust boundaries, and delegating to Zetto for actual linking. The AxonEngine class and plugin interfaces reveal the build-system-like abstraction that coordinates the entire substrate.
focusAreas: AxonEngine architecture — plugin pipeline vs. monolithic linker
resolveTrustBoundary — how trust is modelled as a graph concept
AxonPlugin interface — extension mechanism for the orchestrator
Analogies to Vite/Webpack — why a bundler architecture was chosen for a blockchain settlement layer

steps.rationale: The OKF (Open Knowledge Federation) engine — the single most imported infrastructure module, serving concept read/write/list on port 9002. This is the 'knowledge OS' that all agents and services write to. Understanding the unified okf_bridge + kiro_mcp merge, the concept store abstraction, and integration with s2l_pipeline/embedding_pipeline is essential before any agent or SDK module makes sense.
focusAreas: OKF concept store — the universal persistence abstraction (replaces traditional DB)
Port 9002 HTTP service — why HTTP vs. gRPC/Unix socket for internal knowledge
Integration with embedding_pipeline and s2l_pipeline — the write-side pipeline
How this module replaces multiple separate services (okf_bridge + kiro_mcp)

steps.rationale: Era III Adaptive Layer — demonstrates the system's self-modifying architecture: auto-type generation, skill evolution, self-healing, and feedback learning. This is the first place an architect sees the 'evolutionary' design pattern that distinguishes S7G from static systems. The feedback loop (monitor → analyze → adapt) is a recurring motif across all layers.
focusAreas: Auto-type generator — how the system extends its own type system at runtime
Self-healing engine — anomaly detection → repair spawning pattern
Feedback learner — capturing human edits as training data (active learning loop)
Skill evolution — usage-based improvement without code deploys

steps.rationale: Era V Autonomous Layer — the self-improvement and ethics constraint system. Critical architectural pattern: the system can analyze and modify its own code with rollback safety. The ethics layer constrains autonomous action with human oversight circuit breaker. This represents the 'safety envelope' design for autonomous AI systems.
focusAreas: Self-improvement pipeline — code analysis → modification → rollback
Ethics constraint layer — how autonomous actions are bounded
Continuous evolution loop — the 'never-done' monitoring pattern
Logging to OKF — why all autonomous actions are audited to the knowledge store

steps.rationale: The cross-chain execution bridge — connects portfolio allocations to on-chain execution across web3, CCTP, FedNow, Avalanche, Mercury, and more. The architectural pattern here is a 'gateway with Raft consensus guard' and topological similarity engine, revealing how off-chain orchestration meets on-chain settlement. Critical for understanding the DePIN settlement claim.
focusAreas: Multi-chain abstraction — how different settlement rails are unified
Raft consensus guard — why internal consensus precedes on-chain settlement
Topological similarity engine — graph-based routing across chains
CCTP integration pattern — how Circle's protocol is embedded

steps.rationale: The in-process pub/sub event bus for agent-to-agent communication — threadsafe with AetherDB persistence and polling reader for cross-process events. This is the nervous system connecting all 62 agents. The architectural design (in-process pub/sub + persistent backing) reveals the coupling strategy: agents communicate asynchronously through a shared bus, not through direct calls.
focusAreas: EventBus singleton — why in-process pub/sub (not message queue) was chosen
Pre-registered event chains — security_alert, yield_update, bridge_settlement, agent_action
PollingReader — cross-process event discovery without service mesh
Thread safety design — how concurrent agent writes are serialized

steps.rationale: The Agent Swarm Coordinator — orchestrates 17+ specialized AI agents (26 definitions total, the highest in the codebase). This is where the architecture's 'agent-as-microservice' pattern is defined: each agent has a configurable interval (5s-86400s), separate concerns (liquidity, arbitrage, yield, security), and self-contained lifecycle. Understanding SwarmCoordinator reveals how the 62-agent system scales.
focusAreas: Agent abstraction — how agents are defined (interval, execute, cleanup lifecycle)
SwarmCoordinator — scheduling algorithm and agent registry design
Concern separation — why 17+ specialized agents vs. monolithic orchestrator
Configurable intervals — the time-horizon diversity pattern (sub-second to daily)

steps.rationale: The most-imported file in the entire codebase — a thin wrapper re-exporting HermesSecurityAuditor, HermesCostOptimizer, HermesCapacityPlanner, and HermesEnvironmentManager. Its high import count reveals it as the central dependency hub. The architectural insight is in the 'facade pattern' — why a re-export module becomes the most depended-upon file, and what that says about the coupling structure.
focusAreas: Facade/aggregator pattern — why thin wrappers become dependency hubs
Hermes agent responsibilities — security, cost, capacity, environment as cross-cutting concerns
Module coupling — what hermes_agents.py's import count reveals about architecture
Why this is the most-imported file despite being the simplest

steps.rationale: The base agent class for the entire swarm — provides common lifecycle methods (init/execute/cleanup), configuration loading, logging, and health reporting. This is the abstract base class (ABC) pattern that all 62 agents inherit from. Understanding this class reveals the 'contract' that every agent must fulfill and the framework's extension mechanism.
focusAreas: BaseAgent ABC — the template method pattern for agent lifecycle
Configuration loading strategy — how agents discover their config
Health reporting — the monitoring contract all agents must implement
Logging abstraction — how agent-level observability is standardized

steps.rationale: The Python SDK v2.4 entry point — 83 methods across 5 modules covering the full surface area. For an architect, this is the 'published interface' contract: what external consumers see and how the system's capabilities are packaged. Understanding S7GClient configuration (API base, ICP calls, SIP messaging) reveals the system's boundary design and extension API pattern.
focusAreas: S7GClient — the unified client pattern (analogous to boto3/web3 patterns)
Domain module decomposition — why 5 domains (bridge, solana, yield, agents, base)
ICP call integration — how Internet Computer canisters are called from SDK
API surface design — 83 methods as a measurement of system scope

steps.rationale: The Unified Sovereign Mesh CLI (Era 216.0) — the administrative entry point for all sovereign management operations. For an architect, this reveals the operational interface: systemd service management, key management, Prometheus telemetry, MCP server management, and attestation. The CLI pattern here is the 'converged operations' design — one tool to manage the entire stack.
focusAreas: CLI command tree — how administrative operations are organized
systemd integration — why user services (not containers) for zero-cost infra
Key management design — how sovereign keys are stored and used
MCP server management — the MCP tool protocol as an extension mechanism

steps.rationale: Smart routing for sovereign agent requests — call_provider and smart_route functions that route queries to the optimal LLM provider based on cost, latency, capability, and availability. This is the 'intelligent load balancer' of the agent system and reveals how the architecture handles multi-LLM provider heterogeneity: a router abstraction that decouples agent logic from inference backend.
focusAreas: Provider abstraction — how different LLMs (local, DeepSeek, OpenAI) are unified
Smart routing algorithm — cost/latency/capability/availability as routing dimensions
Fallback chain design — what happens when preferred providers fail
Connection to external_gateway and local_inference — the inference supply chain

steps.rationale: The comprehensive test suite (19 tests, 21 definitions) — exercises the entire stack from agents to infrastructure, stress, and tracing. For an architect, this is the 'integration contract validation': which combinations of subsystems are tested together reveals expected coupling patterns and integration points. The test structure is documentation of how the architecture is meant to work end-to-end.
focusAreas: Test coverage scope — which subsystems are tested together (coupling validation)
t_74 through t_88 naming — what the test numbering reveals about system evolution
Integration test patterns — how agents ↔ bridge ↔ SDK interactions are validated
What's NOT tested — gaps that reveal architectural assumptions

steps.rationale: Capstone file that ties the architectural journey together — the Zetto attestation adapter replaces os.urandom-based attestation hashes with Zetto's Keccak-256 seals, with graceful fallback to Python hashlib. This single file demonstrates the entire architecture's layering: Python orchestration → Rust substrate → cryptographic sealing, all with a fallback mechanism for portability. It's the architectural 'keystone' showing how all layers compose.
focusAreas: Adapter pattern — ZettoAttester as drop-in replacement for os.urandom
Graceful degradation — what happens when libzetto.so is unavailable
seal_transaction → seal_consensus → seal_reserve_attestation — the attestation hierarchy
How this file ties together: Python orchestration → Rust substrate → cryptography
